screens.title: The Impact of One Click
screens.subtitle: Phishing scams can appear in emails, messages, and job platforms. They’re designed to look real and will often rely on urgency and trust.

screens.context: You are a new hire at EY who has just been onboarded onto the team. You receive an URGENT email from someone who’s claiming to be part of the HR team.

interactiveImage.imageAlt: Mock-up of a suspicious HR email in a workplace inbox

screens.question: What is the best course of action?

options.label: Click on the link to verify if this is a phishing scam or not
Hover over the link to see the URL attached and alert management
Send them an email back to start a conversation about the issue

explanations.title: Ignore the message completely
explanations.description: Ignoring the message would still allow scammer to continuing disrupting others. Security teams want these types of emails reported.

explanations.title: Click on the link to verify if this is a phishing scam or not
explanations.description: Clicking a suspicious link can verify interaction and trigger malware damages (depending on the hacker).

explanations.title: Hover over the link to see the URL attached and alert management
explanations.description: Hovering allows you to inspect the link without click and alerting IT if you are suspicious would allow action to be taken.

explanations.title: Send them an email back to start a conversation about the issue
explanations.description: Sending them an email back confirms to the scammer that your email is active.

screens.title: Common Patterns to Identify Phishing

interactiveImage.imageAlt: Clickable mock-up email with suspicious elements

screens.instructions: As you click through suspicious parts of the email, explanations will appear describing why each part is suspicious.

clickableElements.label: Sender address
clickableElements.explanation: The sender may appear to come from HR, but the email address or domain may be slightly altered.

clickableElements.label: Urgent wording
clickableElements.explanation: Phishing scams often use urgency to pressure you into acting quickly without verifying the request.

clickableElements.label: Suspicious link
clickableElements.explanation: Embedded links can lead to fake login pages or malicious websites designed to steal credentials.

clickableElements.label: Unexpected request
clickableElements.explanation: Unexpected requests involving personal, payroll, or login information are a common phishing tactic.

screens.title: What should you do next time?
Module Complete
screens.reflection: Congrats on completing your interactive module!
takeaways: Actions you should take against potential phishing scams
